lines: Butters and Carter are locked together at the top on 49pts each — a genuine title fight, though Carter's already torched Algeria and DR Congo in the group stage so he's essentially running a two-horse race on England and Colombia now. Cosy.

Callum, Wilmot, Al, and Jim are all bunched in the chasing pack between 33–36pts, each with three teams still alive — this is where the tournament gets interesting and someone's going to do something stupid.

Dene, you absolute melt. France is your only surviving team. One bad day for Les Bleus and you're done. Senegal, Bosnia, Tunisia — it's like you drafted a sweepstake team by closing your eyes and spinning a globe with your elbow.

Ellis — still in with Argentina, who look genuinely good, but let's not forget South Korea, Paraguay, and New Zealand also went home early, making her entry basically a one-trick pony. One fuck of a trick though, we'll give her that.

Today's only action is South Africa vs Canada — neither owned by anyone in this sweepstake, so it's purely for vibes — but keep your eyes on the bracket, because whoever emerges could be the banana skin waiting for Butters' Portugal or Carter's England further down the road.
